data.title: These Analysts Revise Their Forecasts On Braze Following Q4 Results
data.description: Braze, Inc. (NASDAQ: BRZE) exceeded expectations in the fourth quarter by reporting a revenue of $205.17 million and introducing a $100 million share repurchase plan. The company fell short on adjusted earnings, posting ten cents per share compared to the anticipated 14 cents. CEO Bill Magnuson highlighted the successful Q4, marking the third consecutive quarter of increased organic revenue growth and solid operating leverage. For fiscal 2027, Braze anticipates adjusted EPS between 61 to 65 cents, differing from the analyst estimate of 63 cents, with revenue expected to fall between $884 million to $889 million, surpassing the $857.25 million prediction. Following the news, Braze's shares rose by 19.9%, closing at $21.60. Analysts, including Piper Sandler's Billy Fitzsimmons, adjusted their price targets for Braze post-earnings report. More details on this story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Madison Capital Group Strategies Now Available on iCapital Marketplace
data.description: Madison Capital Group, a real estate investment firm located in Charlotte, North Carolina, revealed on March 25, 2026, that three of its real estate investment plans can now be accessed through iCapital Marketplace. This platform serves as a technology-based solution for wealth managers and advisors to engage with various investment opportunities.

data.title: New Chip Technology at Argonne Enables Real-Time Insights From Scientific Data
data.description: In Lemont, Illinois, a cutting-edge chip developed by Argonne is revolutionizing data processing for scientists. This innovative chip has the ability to compress and process detector data in real-time, enabling researchers to analyze results and direct experiments concurrently. This technology significantly enhances the speed and efficiency of data analysis, ultimately improving the scientific research process.

data.title: FunL Celebrates Nearly One Year on Amazon With Thriving Sales, Set to Debut at National Hardware Show 2026
data.description: A Stretchable Automotive Funnel with a patented design has become popular among DIYers and professionals alike. CKD Solutions will showcase this innovative product at the National Hardware Show 2026 in Las Vegas at Booth #1667. FunL, the company behind the funnel, is excited to present their creation.

data.title: Largest workers' comp insurer in Colorado issues $15 million general dividend to nearly 50,000 businesses, encourages reinvestment in Colorado's economy
data.description: Starting March 25, eligible Pinnacol Assurance members, totaling 46,000 businesses, will begin receiving dividend checks. This distribution includes $15 million in general dividends. Notably, this marks the 11th consecutive year that Pinnacol has issued a general dividend, bringing the total amount distributed to $905 million over the years.

data.title: TELA Bio Analysts Cut Their Forecasts After Q4 Results
data.description: TELA Bio, Inc. (NASDAQ: TELA) released its fourth-quarter results, showcasing a reported loss of 17 cents per share, surpassing analysts' forecast of an 18 cents per share loss. Despite this beat, the company's quarterly sales of $20.869 million fell short of the anticipated $21.047 million. Looking ahead, TELA Bio projects first-quarter sales to reach $18.500 million, below market expectations of $21.277 million. Antony Koblish, Co-Founder and CEO, highlighted the company's positive performance, noting a 18% revenue growth to $20.9 million in Q4 2024, achieved while maintaining operational expenses stable throughout the year. Koblish expressed confidence in the firm's operational efficiency for the future.

data.title: Rio Tinto’s Diavik diamond mine delivers its final production
data.description: In Yellowknife, Canada, Rio Tinto's Diavik diamond mine in the Northwest Territories has concluded its operations after 23 years, yielding over 150 million carats of rough diamonds before depleting its economic reserves. The Managing Director of Rio Tinto Iron & Titanium and Diamonds, Sophie Bergeron, reflected on the mine's significance, noting the initial skepticism about Canada's diamond potential four decades ago. Bergeron highlighted the mine's impactful legacy on the industry and region.

data.title: Stock Market Today: Small Caps, Techs Lead Gains; This Nuclear Play Breaks Out But Micron Sinks (Live Coverage)
data.description: The Dow Jones index saw a rise today as President Donald Trump presented a peace plan to Iran. Meanwhile, oil prices experienced a significant decline. Small caps and tech stocks led the gains in the market, with a noticeable breakout in a nuclear play. However, Micron faced a decline in its stock value on this active trading day.

data.title: CoolIT Employees Share in $4.75 Billion Transaction as KKR Agrees to Sell to Ecolab
data.description: KKR, a prominent global investment firm, has revealed the sale of CoolIT Systems, a key player in liquid data center cooling, to Ecolab in a deal worth $4.75 billion. This transaction stands as one of KKR's significant recent accomplishments, resulting in about 15 times the initial equity invested, including distributions. Upon completion, all CoolIT employees will transition to Ecolab.

data.title: Better Home & Finance Holding Company Appoints Hugh Frater, Former Fannie Mae CEO and BlackRock Founding Partner, to Board of Directors
data.description: Better Home & Finance Holding Company (NASDAQ: BETR) disclosed the appointment of Hugh Frater to its Board of Directors, effective March 23, 2026. Frater, a founding partner at BlackRock and the former CEO of Fannie Mae, joins the leading AI-powered homeownership company to provide expertise in capital markets, housing finance, and corporate management. His vast experience is anticipated to play a crucial role in steering Better in its long-term strategy.

data.title: Advance Auto Parts to Feature Full Paint Scheme on Ryan Blaney-piloted No. 12 Team Penske Ford Mustang Dark Horse in Four NASCAR Cup Series Races
data.description: Raleigh, North Carolina - Advance Auto Parts, Inc. (NYSE: AAP), a top automotive aftermarket parts supplier in North America catering to professionals and DIY customers, revealed that Team Penske's No. 12 Ford Mustang, driven by Ryan Blaney, will showcase the complete Advance paint design at four NASCAR Cup Series events this year. The Advance branding will be prominently featured on both the No. 12 car and the Team Penske crew fire suits during The Cook Out 400 at Martin.

data.title: Navan Stock Soars On Q4 Earnings Beat
data.description: Shares of Navan Inc (NASDAQ: NAVN) are on the rise in the after-hours trading session following the company's fourth-quarter financial report. Navan stock is experiencing significant growth, with fourth-quarter revenue reported at $177.92 million, surpassing analyst predictions of $162 million. The company also exceeded expectations in adjusted earnings, reporting two cents per share compared to the estimated loss of 12 cents per share. Total revenue saw a 35% increase, driving NAVN stock to new heights. For more details, the complete story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: These Analysts Increase Their Forecasts On AAR Following Upbeat Q3 Results
data.description: AAR Corp (NYSE: AIR) exceeded expectations in the third quarter, reporting earnings of $1.25 per share, beating the estimated $1.15 per share. Quarterly sales were $845.1 million, surpassing the projected $812.5 million. The company raised its fiscal year 2026 sales forecast following a 25% increase in total sales, including 14% organic adjusted sales growth. John M. Holmes, Chairman, President, and CEO of AAR, expressed satisfaction with the quarterly performance, highlighting growth in parts, repair, and software platform activities. The Parts Supply segment saw a 45% increase, propelled by a 36% organic growth in the new parts Distribution activity.

data.title: Unchained Launches Journey Works AI: The First AI Operating System Purpose-Built for Destination Marketing Organizations
data.description: Today, Unchained, an AI consulting firm based in Chicago, introduced Journey Works AI, the initial artificial intelligence system tailored for the unique operations of destination marketing entities and convention & visitors bureaus (CVBs). Unlike traditional chatbots or CRM add-ons, Journey Works AI stands out as a comprehensive AI platform featuring more than 50 specialized agents. These agents are dedicated to automating routine operational tasks within such organizations.
